items.title: School told to find and exhume graves ahead of planned development

children.content: A Catholic school in northern Tasmania can apply to build new facilities over a former cemetery after a government office declared it "not a cemetery".

gallery.altText: A school playground on a foggy day.
A sign out the front of Our Lady of Mercy Catholic School in Deloraine

thumbnail.altText: A school playground on a foggy day.
A sign out the front of Our Lady of Mercy Catholic School in Deloraine

items.title: 'I apologise': Tasmanian premier pledges reforms after Ogilvie legal saga

children.content: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff has apologised and pledged to strengthen transparency around the use of public funds for politicians' legal proceedings, following a minister's resignation.

image.altText: A man in a suit stands walks into a fancy living room amongst others

items.title: Try before you buy: E-bike borrow program driving sales

children.content: A free e-bike library is set to continue in Hobart after a successful first year and a reported rise in sales, as more people make the switch to e-bikes.

image.altText: A man smiles for photo in a bike store, resting his hand on his e-bike

items.title: Hobart stadium future not an issue at AFL meeting, ABC told

children.content: Despite suggestions that potential delays and cost blowouts of Tasmania's new stadium may be on the minds of AFL heads, the topic was barely mentioned at the AFL's annual meeting.

gallery.altText: An aerial view od Hobart's waterfront, with an artist's impression of the stadium and new housing.
Two football players celebrate during a game.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial view od Hobart's waterfront, with an artist's impression of the stadium and new housing.
Two football players celebrate during a game.

items.title: 'Everything's on hold': Patients caught in surgery backlog

children.content: Patients are waiting years for elective surgery as delays and cancellations mount in Tasmania — can the health system handle the $700 million of cuts still to come?

image.altText: A person in surgical scrubs, mask and gloves hands a pair of gloves to another person in scrubs without gloves on.

items.title: How did Annie survive nine days in the 'shocking' Pyengana bush in the winter of 1908?

children.content: Annie Beechey's great-granddaughter recalls the story of how she survived and how she was eventually found.

image.altText: Annie Beechey

items.title: Escaped devil spotted on suburban Gold Coast CCTV after week on loose

children.content: A two-year-old Tasmanian devil has been on the loose since last Tuesday when she leapt out of her enclosure at a Gold Coast wildlife park.

image.altText: Tasmanian devil found in cctv.

items.title: Tony Abbott says Liberal voters feel 'let down' by successive Coalition governments

children.content: Former prime minister Tony Abbott has told a book launch in Washington that Liberal voters feel "let down" by successive Coalition governments. Follow live.

image.altText: Tony Abbott

items.title: Why was Rolf Harris never tried for his crimes in Australia?

children.content: Rolf Harris's conviction for child sexual abuse was shocking to those who saw him as a lovable larrikin. But the people who were abused by him are refusing to stay silent about the impacts of his crimes.

gallery.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

thumbnail.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

items.title: How police may have inadvertently saved this man from Ivan Milat

children.content: Etched in Steven Clark's memory are the protruding teeth and moustache of the driver he says picked him up as a teenager — a man he now believes was serial killer Ivan Milat.

image.altText: Man wearing a hat stands on the side of a road.

items.title: These cranes are battery-powered as firms increasingly ditch diesel

children.content: The Iran war and the oil supply squeeze are supercharging business investment in renewables.

image.altText: Crane in the centre of a large construction site. City buildings in the background.

items.title: Why Xi Jinping made an exception to see Kim Jong Un in person

children.content: Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un's bilateral talks in North Korea were a big departure from the Chinese president's usual diplomacy playbook because, for this first time this year, he was the one travelling abroad to make the meeting happen.

image.altText: Two men wearing suits seen sitting and smiling in the crowd at a stadium.

items.title: Five mattresses on the floor as families wait months for repairs

children.content: Delays in maintenance repairs, including mould removal, are forcing families to sleep on mattresses in living rooms, adding to overcrowding pressures in remote homes.

image.altText: A woman standing in a kitchen holding a baby.

items.title: Is Australia sucking the joy out of childhood?

children.content: The first time Renae Powell waved her eldest child through the primary school gates she felt something was missing: playtime.

gallery.altText: Two young white kids holding hands, running through long grass
Two children grasp an overhead rope as they balance on slipper, mud-covered concrete tubes between two gum trees.

thumbnail.altText: Two young white kids holding hands, running through long grass
Two children grasp an overhead rope as they balance on slipper, mud-covered concrete tubes between two gum trees.

items.title: Worker questions security response at Sydney shopping centre

children.content: A retail worker at Sydney's Broadway Shopping Centre says she was left scared after a man told her he had a knife.

image.altText: Shivanya in grey top holding bag outside shopping centre

items.title: Serena Williams makes winning return to court at Queen's

children.content: Serena Williams steals the show at Queen's Club, as the American icon marks her eagerly awaited return to competitive action with a doubles victory nearly four years after her last match.

image.altText: Serena Williams celebrates a point with Victoria Mboko at Queen's.

items.title: Finding and saving the world's biggest trees before they disappear

children.content: It took a decade for researchers to find the tallest tree in East Asia. Now they know where the 84.1-metre conifer is but the future of its species and other tall trees is dire.

gallery.altText: Man in orange jumper and climbing ropes prays with tree through hugging

thumbnail.altText: Man in orange jumper and climbing ropes prays with tree through hugging

items.title: Quiz: Stow your luggage in the locker overhead, we're off to see the world

children.content: The lek is the currency of which country? And what's the Japanese word for pufferfish, the sashimi delicacy that's toxic if prepared incorrectly?

image.altText: Chop sticks hover over a plat of thinly sliced raw white fish with a green garnish.

items.title: Calling all budding storytellers living with disability

children.content: If you are an emerging content maker or journalist with disability and have a story to tell, now is your chance to work with the ABC to tell it.

image.altText: two women stand alongside one another smiling. One of the women has her right arm amputated. They stand in an ABC studio.

items.title: When does the FIFA World Cup start and who are the favourites?

children.content: The FIFA World Cup is the biggest single-sport event on the planet and has delivered numerous historical moments, from Diego Maradona's "hand of God" in 1986 to Zinedine Zidane's infamous headbutt on Marco Materazzi.

gallery.altText: A smiling man lifts a golden trophy among celebrating teammates with fireworks exploding in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A smiling man lifts a golden trophy among celebrating teammates with fireworks exploding in the background.

items.title: Man found dead in Melbourne park had suffered burns, police say

children.content: Police say the man appeared to have suffered burns and was discovered by a passer-by in parkland in Melbourne's east.

image.altText: A police vehicle.

items.title: Victim-survivors tear up agreement with Catholic church

children.content: Victim-survivors of clergy abuse say negotiations and agreements reached with the Catholic Diocese of Ballarat are now null and void.

image.altText: Colourful ribbons on a church ground fence

items.title: Gary Ablett Jr joins dad in Australian Football Hall of Fame

children.content: Six new members are inducted into the Australian Football Hall of Fame, including Geelong and Gold Coast legend Gary Ablett Jr.

image.altText: Gary Ablett Jr in a tuxedo seen side-on with his name on a screen behind him.

items.title: Kyrgios wins Stuttgart opener after five-month tour absence

children.content: Nick Kyrgios makes a successful return to the court, claiming only his second singles victory since 2022.

image.altText: Nick Kyrgios prepares to play a forehand on the run at Stuttgart ATP Tour event.

items.title: 'Just an absolute tip': Residents frustrated with rise of illegal dumping

children.content: Councils across Melbourne's suburbs are struggling to combat the rise of illegal dumping, despite over $21 million of funding from the Victorian state government.

items.title: Western Sydney International Airport opening date announced

children.content: After 15 years of planning, seven years of construction and a year of testing, Western Sydney International Airport has confirmed its opening date for passengers.

image.altText: An aerial view of the new Western Sydney Airport taken by a drone.

items.title: How a 'bit of paint' could help relieve overcrowded Brisbane buses

children.content: Buses are Queensland's most popular form of public transport, but advocates say a lack of investment means they are often unreliable and overcrowded.

image.altText: People waiting for a bus

items.title: Mystery streak of light from China gives Australians spectacular show

children.content: Some skywatchers describe a silvery or blue-tinted ball of gas that gradually turned red and disappeared.

image.altText: Bright silver burst in sky

items.title: Steaks could soon cost more, but restaurants have other ideas

children.content: Red meat is expected to become more expensive starting this month as higher diesel prices have filtered through the supply chain.

image.altText: A juicy rib steak on a bone served on a restaurant plate with a sophisticated salad and glaze.

items.title: Seized greyhounds looked like 'RSPCA cases', kennel owner says

children.content: A dog kennel owner is searching for answers after Greyhound Racing SA put two dogs that appeared malnourished and one with an open wound "you could stick your finger into" in her care.
Warning: This article contains images and content that some readers may find distressing.

image.altText: A woman in a pink sweatshirt patting a greyhound.

items.title: Aussies record first ODI loss to Bangladesh in 21 years

children.content: Bangladesh posts just its second victory over Australia in 23 ODIs, recording an 86-run win in the opening match of the series in Dhaka.

image.altText: Liam Scott reacts while batting for Australia against Bangladesh in a 2026 ODI.

items.title: NASA announces Artemis III crew

children.content: NASA has revealed the crew for its Artemis III mission, the next step in the US space agency's plan to eventually land astronauts on the moon.
